title: Vanilla Excelled
description: Enchancing Minecraft without going too overboard.
body:
I've always loved Minecraft. But after playing for so long, starting a new vanilla world starts to feel like every other world you've ever made. You boot it up, play for a couple hours, log off — and never open it again.

I made this modpack to fix that.

# Why this modpack?

The goal was simple: **enhance** vanilla without making it feel like a completely different game. No crazy dimensions, no overpowered tech mods — just Minecraft, but the way it *should* feel in 2026.

## A World Worth Exploring

We haven't had a real biome update in over 10 years. And honestly? That really sucks. This modpack tackles that head-on, bringing life back to a world that's felt the same for way too long. On top of that, **200+ mods** and **20 resource packs** work together to make everything feel fresh — without losing that vanilla charm.

## Newer features, older Minecraft

It also backports some of the best new and upcoming features — including **Spring to Life**, the **Copper Age**, and the **Happy Ghast update**. And villagers? They've finally gotten a much needed buff, because with **Liberty's Villagers** they're much more intelligent.

## Playable on anything

And here's the thing about other modpacks — they either add too many mods, or barely change anything at all. Worse, almost none of them bother with more than a handful of **performance mods**, meaning half the player base gets left out. I made sure that wasn't the case here. This modpack is built to run on **90% of computers**, so your 2-week Minecraft phase doesn't have to end because of bad FPS.

**This is still Vanilla. Just Excelled.**

Credits to [Vanilla Tweaks!](https://vanillatweaks.net/)
and [Liberty's Villagers Revived](https://www.curseforge.com/minecraft/mc-mods/libertys-villagers-revived)
